key_insight: A two-stage evolutionary search can make PINN training more accurate and robust by using cheap low-fidelity screening first, then fully training only the most promising hyperparameter settings.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical way to tune PINNs for PDE solvers under fixed compute budgets, improving reliability for physics and engineering simulation workflows.
method: Use an evolutionary algorithm to explore mixed hyperparameters in a black-box outer loop.
Run truncated low-epoch PINN trainings to cheaply rank candidate configurations.
Fully train the top candidates with standard gradient-based optimization in stage two.
evidence_signal: The approach was evaluated on Advection, Klein-Gordon, and Helmholtz PDE benchmarks and reported consistently lower mean error than standard training under constrained computational resources.
watch_item: The method still depends on the quality of the low-fidelity screening stage, so poor early rankings or insufficient budget allocation could miss the best PINN configuration.
paper_title: Evolutionary Two-Stage Hyperparameter Optimization Strategies for Physics-Informed Neural Networks
